question: When Dad lived with his parents, what did he used to do at the meal?
choices: Attempt to merge the soup bubbles into one big bubble using a fork
Practice variations of his "one fake dumpling" joke in Mandarin
Hone an ambidextrous use of the chopsticks
Try to use as much English as possible

question: Ye Ye's favorite story to tell about Dad's childhood is that Dad...
choices: Played his hand in the linoleum and held up the other hand to indicate how he would eat
Was injured by another kid who swung around a nail / wood plank and recieved a bag of apology oranges
Subtly got water on those below the balcony in Taiwan when it was already raining
Used a washboard as a makeshift bridge across a "large" septic pit

question: When Facebook first IPO'd, Dad...
choices: Sold it for cash and gave large amounts to Christian charities
Acquired a family yacht and named it "Active II" (is Active I still sailing?)
Invested it in a single stablecoin suggested by his friends
Did nothing. He still owns these shares and thus "diamond-handed" himself to richness.

question: Back in the Kirkland era, Dad once took Caleb to:
choices: Portland, where we ate Pepperidge Farm cookies from the reception
Singapore, where the kids ordered sago malacca wherever it was offered
Great Wolf Lodge, where we still couldn't figure out the difference between fudge and one other thing
Home Depot; we then made lightsabers that would have been wildly too big for ~4 year old Caleb

question: Dad once upgraded the family's long-haul flight to:
choices: Japan Airlines: You Must be Tired from Your Long Journey Here~ Class
A SpaceX charter mission with two loops around the sun

question: When Dad tested inflatably deploying cycle helmets at the London office, the results were:
choices: concerning. Deployment was non-deterministic even for point-blank falls onto local bean bags.
paradigm-shifting, inspiring weekly office boxing rounds promoting the reliable headgear
litigiously caustic. Dad stepped down in the wake of recursive counter-suits for false advertising and defamation.
never obtained, because all of Dad's 500+ employees found the helmets too uncomfortable around the neck

question: Caleb once saw Dad in a towel and eventually changed the topic to:
choices: the role of mantissas and exponents in floating-point arithmetic
dominant and recessive genetics ("why the flowers mostly turned out red")
simulating binary computations with a subset of the standard logic gates
Caleb's own suspicion that he might have a form of Asperger's
